Doctor_English: Are you having any chest pain?
Patient_Yoruba: Àyà mi ń dùn mí nígbà tí mo bá mí, ó sì máa ń gbóná fún mi láti mí púpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: My chest hurts when I breathe, and it's difficult for me to take deep breaths.
Direct_Translation_English: Chest my is aching me when I am breathing, it also usually is hot for me to breathe much.

Doctor_English: We might need to adjust your medication dosage. Do you have any concerns?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo nírètí pé kò ní ní ipa búburú lórí mi ju èyí tó wà tẹ́lẹ̀ lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: I hope it won't have more negative side effects on me than before.
Direct_Translation_English: I hope that it will not have impact bad on me more than this that was before.

Doctor_English: Are you experiencing any numbness or tingling sensations?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ni, ọwọ́ àti ẹsẹ̀ mi máa ń dọ̀ọ́dọ̀ọ́ bí ẹni pé ìka ń já mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Yes, my hands and feet often feel numb/tingly, as if pins and needles are pricking me.
Direct_Translation_English: Yes, hands and feet my usually are numb-numb like one that pins is picking me.

Doctor_English: How many times have you vomited today?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti bì nígbà mẹ́ta báyìí, kò sì sí ohunkóhun tó dúró nínú mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: I have vomited three times now, and nothing stays down.
Direct_Translation_English: I have vomited times three now, not and no anything that stays inside me.

Doctor_English: Are you experiencing any morning sickness?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ti pọ̀ jù. Mo máa ń pọ́nkí ní òwúrọ̀, kò sì fẹ́ tẹ́ mi lọ́rùn. Èmi kò fẹ́ jẹun.
Clinical_Translation_English: It's too much. I usually feel nauseous in the morning, and I don't want to eat.
Direct_Translation_English: It has much too much. I usually feel nauseous in morning, not also want satisfy me throat. I not want eat.

Doctor_English: Do you have any difficulty walking or balancing?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ni, ẹsẹ̀ mi kì í fẹ́ gbé mi dáradára, mo sì máa ń gbọ̀n púpọ̀ nígbà gbogbo.
Clinical_Translation_English: Yes, my legs don't support me well, and I often feel very shaky.
Direct_Translation_English: Yes, leg my not will want carry me well, I also often shake much in time all.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any changes in your skin, like rashes or itching?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, ara mi ń yọ tànràn-tànràn, ó sì ń yún mi gan-an. Mo ti pa á títí ó fi bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí í jẹ́ẹran.
Clinical_Translation_English: Yes, I have a rash, and it's very itchy. I've scratched it until it started to bleed.
Direct_Translation_English: Yes, body my is producing rash, it also is itching me very. I have scratched it until it started to hurt-skin.

Doctor_English: Are you currently taking any medications for your blood pressure?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, mo ń mu òògùn tó kọ́kọ́ sọ fún mi, ṣùgbọ́n mo gbàgbé orúkọ rẹ̀ báyìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Yes, I am taking the initial medication prescribed to me, but I've forgotten its name now.
Direct_Translation_English: Yes indeed, I am taking medicine that first you said for me, but I forgot name its now.

Doctor_English: What exactly happened? Did you fall?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, mo ṣubú láti orí alùpùpù. Ẹsẹ̀ mi kùnà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Yes, I fell off a motorcycle. My leg is fractured.
Direct_Translation_English: Yes, I fell from head motorcycle. Leg my failed.

Doctor_English: Are you feeling nauseous, even if you haven't vomited?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ni, inú mi ń bì lọ́nà kan, bí ẹni pé ó fẹ́rẹ̀ẹ́ jáde.
Clinical_Translation_English: Yes, I feel nauseous, as if I am about to vomit.
Direct_Translation_English: Yes, my stomach is churning in a way, like it is almost coming out.

Doctor_English: When was your last menstrual period?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó tó oṣù méjì sẹ́yìn tí àdéhùn mi kẹ́yìn. Mo fẹ́ kí ẹ yẹ̀ mí wò fún oyún.
Clinical_Translation_English: My last period was two months ago. I'd like you to check me for pregnancy.
Direct_Translation_English: It is two months ago that appointment my was last. I want that you check me for pregnancy.

Doctor_English: Good morning, what brings you to the hospital today?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ káàárọ̀, mo ní ibà gíga àti ara gbígbóná púpọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Good morning, I have a high fever and my body is very hot.
Direct_Translation_English: Good morning, I have fever high and body hot much.

Doctor_English: Are you able to pass stool normally?
Patient_Yoruba: Rárá, Dókítà. Ó máa ń le, ó sì máa ń gba àkókò púpọ̀ kí n tó lè yọ ọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: No, Doctor. It's usually hard, and it takes a long time before I can pass it.
Direct_Translation_English: No, Doctor. It usually is hard, it also usually takes time much before I can expel it.
